stem:
w(d) = −0.031(d/30)² + 6.42(d/30) + 12.5

The function w gives the predicted weight w(d), in kilograms (kg), of a certain animal d days after it was born, where d ≤ 420. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the statement "w(180) is approximately equal to 47" in this context?

answerOptions.content: The predicted weight of the animal was approximately 180 kg 47 days after it was born.
The predicted weight of the animal was approximately 47 kg 180 days after it was born.
The predicted weight of the animal was approximately 47 kg 180/30 days after it was born.
The predicted weight of the animal was approximately 180/30 kg 47 days after it was born.

rationale: Choice B is correct. The input 180 is the value of d (days), and the output approximately 47 is the predicted weight in kg. So the weight was approximately 47 kg, 180 days after birth. Choice A swaps the meaning. Choices C and D misinterpret the units.
